---
title: "PLSR Model Report"
subtitle: "Partial Least Squares Regression Analysis"
format: 
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry:
      - top=20mm
      - left=20mm
      - right=20mm
      - bottom=20mm
    fontsize: 11pt
    fig-pos: 'H'
    keep-tex: false
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
params:
  author: "SpecTraits User"
  date: !expr Sys.Date()
  trait_selector: "trait"
  split_method: NULL
  press_method: NULL
  press_frame: NULL
  final_method: NULL
  final_PLSR: NULL
  results_predict: NULL
  build_import_figure: NULL
  split_action_figure: NULL
  press_action_figure: NULL
  coefficients_figure: NULL
  perf_train_figure: NULL
  perf_test_figure: NULL
---

# Report Information

**Trait of Interest:** `r params$trait_selector`  
**Author:** `r params$author`  
**Date:** `r format(params$date, "%B %d, %Y")`  
**Analysis Method:** Partial Least Squares Regression (PLSR)

---

# Executive Summary

This report presents the results of a Partial Least Squares Regression (PLSR) model developed to predict **`r params$trait_selector`** from leaf spectral reflectance data.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| include: false

library(data.table)
results_dt <- as.data.table(params$results_predict)
```

**Key Findings:**

- Total samples: **`r nrow(params$results_predict)`**
- Training samples: **`r nrow(results_dt[Dataset == "Training"])`**
- Testing samples: **`r nrow(results_dt[Dataset == "Testing"])`**
- Optimal components: **`r params$final_method$ncomp`**

\newpage

# Model Configuration

## Sample Distribution

The dataset was partitioned into training and testing subsets.

## Model Features

- **PLSR Method**: `r params$final_method$method`
- **Number of Components**: `r params$final_method$ncomp`
- **Iterations**: `r params$final_method$iterations`
- **Split Method**: `r params$split_method$split`

\newpage

# Data Exploration

## Trait and Spectra Visualization

```{r}
#| label: fig-import-spectra
#| fig-cap: "Spectral signatures"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$build_import_figure$spectra_figure())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-import-trait
#| fig-cap: "Trait distribution"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$build_import_figure$trait_figure())
```

\newpage

# Data Split Visualization 

```{r}
#| label: fig-data-split-spectra
#| fig-cap: "Spectral data split showing training and testing samples"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$split_action_figure$spectra_split_figure())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-data-split-trait
#| fig-cap: "Trait distribution for training and testing samples"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$split_action_figure$trait_split_figure())
```

\newpage

# PLSR Model Development
## Component Selection

The optimal number of components was determined using cross-validation.

Optimal number of components: `r params$final_method$ncomp`

```{r}
#| label: fig-press-statistics
#| fig-cap: "PRESS statistics for optimal components"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$press_action_figure())
```

\newpage

## Model Coefficients and VIP

The PLSR regression coefficients and Variable Importance in Projection (VIP) scores.


```{r}
#| label: fig-coefficients
#| fig-cap: "PLSR regression coefficients"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$coefficients_figure$coefficients())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-vip
#| fig-cap: "Variable Importance in Projection (VIP) scores"
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$coefficients_figure$vip())
```

\newpage

## Model Performance Assessment

### Training Dataset Performance

```{r}
#| label: fig-training-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Observed vs. predicted values for training dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_train_figure$obser_pred())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-training-histogram
#| fig-cap: "Distribution comparison for training dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_train_figure$histogram())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-training-residuals
#| fig-cap: "Residuals plot for training dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_train_figure$residuals())
```

\newpage

### Testing Dataset Performance

```{r}
#| label: fig-testing-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Observed vs. predicted values for testing dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_test_figure$obser_pred())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-testing-histogram
#| fig-cap: "Distribution comparison for testing dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_test_figure$histogram())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-testing-residuals
#| fig-cap: "Residuals plot for testing dataset"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

print(params$perf_test_figure$residuals())
```

\newpage

### Performance Metrics Summary

```{r}
#| label: tbl-performance-metrics
#| tbl-cap: "Model performance metrics"
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(knitr)

# Get performance metrics from the performance figures
train_perf <- params$perf_train_figure$performance()
test_perf <- params$perf_test_figure$performance()

# Extract values based on method
if (params$final_method$method == "permutation") {
  # For permutation method, use Mean and SD columns
  perf_table <- data.frame(
    Metric = train_perf$Parameter,
    Training_Mean = train_perf$Mean,
    Training_SD = train_perf$SD,
    Testing_Mean = test_perf$Mean,
    Testing_SD = test_perf$SD
  )
} else {
  # For other methods, use final and SD columns
  perf_table <- data.frame(
    Metric = train_perf$Parameter,
    Training = train_perf$final,
    Training_SD = train_perf$SD,
    Testing = test_perf$final,
    Testing_SD = test_perf$SD
  )
}

kable(perf_table, align = c('l', 'c', 'c', 'c', 'c'), booktabs = TRUE)
```

\newpage

# Software Information

**SpecTraits: Shiny application for spectral trait prediction**

If you use this model or SpecTraits in your research, please cite:

Citation

Guzmán Q., J. Antonio (2025). SpecTraits: A Shiny Application for Prediction of Leaf Traits from Spectral Models. GitHub: ASCEND-BII/SpecTraits

Contact: "aguzman@fas.harvard.edu"

Report Generated: `r format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")`
